compression.original_problem: How can I improve adaptive compression by choosing different compressors for different file chunks?
compression.core_question: How can I improve adaptive compression by choosing different compressors for different file chunks?

variables.name: entropy
variables.description: Randomness or unpredictability of a chunk.

variables.name: repetition
variables.description: Amount of repeated local or global patterns.

variables.name: chunk type
variables.description: Text, binary, tensor, image, archive, log, or mixed data.

variables.name: chunk size
variables.description: Granularity at which decisions are made.

variables.name: compression ratio
variables.description: Compressed size divided by original size.

variables.name: compression/decompression time
variables.description: Runtime cost of encoding and decoding.

variables.name: metadata overhead
variables.description: Header/model information required to decode chunks.

abstract_schema.name: Adaptive Expert Selection
abstract_schema.description: Different input regions require different specialized models, tools, or policies.
abstract_schema.pattern: input -> feature extraction -> hidden type/regime inference -> expert/policy selection -> feedback
abstract_schema.reusable_structure: input -> feature extraction -> hidden type/regime inference -> expert/policy selection -> feedback

similar_schemas.similarity_reason: Shares the structure: input -> feature extraction -> hidden type/regime inference -> expert/policy selection -> feedback
Shares the structure: input -> feature extraction -> hidden type/regime inference -> expert/policy selection -> feedback
Shares the structure: input -> feature extraction -> hidden type/regime inference -> expert/policy selection -> feedback
Shares the structure: input -> feature extraction -> hidden type/regime inference -> expert/policy selection -> feedback
Shares the structure: input -> feature extraction -> hidden type/regime inference -> expert/policy selection -> feedback

transferred_ideas.imported_mechanism: Use a lightweight gating model to route inputs to specialized experts.
transferred_ideas.target_domain_application: Train a lightweight gating model that routes chunks to specialized compressor experts.
transferred_ideas.expected_benefit: Better size/time tradeoff by matching each chunk to the most suitable policy.
transferred_ideas.risk_or_limitation: Routing metadata and classifier errors may erase compression gains.

transferred_ideas.imported_mechanism: Use subtype-specific intervention logic instead of a one-size-fits-all policy.
transferred_ideas.target_domain_application: Classify chunks into subtypes and apply subtype-specific compression policies rather than a global default.
transferred_ideas.expected_benefit: Better size/time tradeoff by matching each chunk to the most suitable policy.
transferred_ideas.risk_or_limitation: Routing metadata and classifier errors may erase compression gains.

transferred_ideas.imported_mechanism: Estimate candidate plan cost before committing to full execution.
transferred_ideas.target_domain_application: Sample each chunk, estimate compressed size/time for candidate compressors, then choose the lowest expected cost before full compression.
transferred_ideas.expected_benefit: Better size/time tradeoff by matching each chunk to the most suitable policy.
transferred_ideas.risk_or_limitation: Routing metadata and classifier errors may erase compression gains.

transferred_ideas.imported_mechanism: Borrow resource-aware scheduling and fallback policies under constraints.
transferred_ideas.target_domain_application: Treat compression time, decompression time, memory, and size as schedulable resources with fallback policies.
transferred_ideas.expected_benefit: Better size/time tradeoff by matching each chunk to the most suitable policy.
transferred_ideas.risk_or_limitation: Routing metadata and classifier errors may erase compression gains.

transferred_ideas.imported_mechanism: Detect hidden regimes and switch policies when the regime changes.
transferred_ideas.target_domain_application: Detect distribution shifts inside large files and switch compression policy at regime boundaries.
transferred_ideas.expected_benefit: Better size/time tradeoff by matching each chunk to the most suitable policy.
transferred_ideas.risk_or_limitation: Routing metadata and classifier errors may erase compression gains.

verification_plan.method: Compare selector against fixed compressors and oracle.
verification_plan.success_condition: Selector improves Pareto frontier or approaches oracle.

verification_plan.method: Predict compressed size/time from samples and features.
verification_plan.success_condition: Low error relative to actual compression.

compression.best_next_step: Implement a small feature extractor plus routing baseline, then compare it against fixed compressors and an oracle on your existing dataset.

trading.original_problem: How can a trading system adapt its strategy when market behavior changes?
trading.core_question: How can a trading system adapt its strategy when market behavior changes?

variables.name: volatility
variables.description: Magnitude and instability of price changes.

variables.name: liquidity
variables.description: Ability to enter/exit positions without large price impact.

variables.name: hidden market regime
variables.description: Latent state such as trending, mean-reverting, crisis, or calm.

variables.name: strategy choice
variables.description: Selected trading rule or portfolio policy.

abstract_schema.name: Regime-Switching Policy Selection
abstract_schema.description: A system changes behavior across hidden states, requiring state inference and policy changes.
abstract_schema.pattern: observations -> hidden state -> selected policy -> outcome -> state update
abstract_schema.reusable_structure: observations -> hidden state -> selected policy -> outcome -> state update

variables.description: Lattice vibrations that can mediate pairing.

variables.name: electron-phonon coupling
variables.description: Interaction strength between electrons and lattice vibrations.

variables.name: pressure
variables.description: External constraint used to stabilize phases.

variables.name: dopants
variables.description: Elements introduced to modify structure or carriers.

AI agents.original_problem: How can an AI agent choose the right tool or model for a user task?
AI agents.core_question: How can an AI agent choose the right tool or model for a user task?

variables.name: user intent
variables.description: Goal implied by the user request.

variables.name: task type
variables.description: Math, coding, search, writing, planning, vision, etc.

variables.name: available tools
variables.description: APIs, databases, solvers, browsers, code execution, or models.

variables.name: latency/cost budget
variables.description: Time and money allowed for a response.

abstract_schema.name: Cost-Based Planning
abstract_schema.description: Estimate the cost of candidate strategies before committing to execution.
abstract_schema.pattern: candidate plans -> estimated cost -> selected plan -> measured result -> updated estimator
abstract_schema.reusable_structure: candidate plans -> estimated cost -> selected plan -> measured result -> updated estimator

transferred_ideas.expected_benefit: Higher task success with lower unnecessary tool cost and better verification.
